The MOUSE action at row 16, col 41 (right eye socket) didn't move the blue squares. They're still at:

- Right: rows 54-58, cols 44-48
So clicking on the right eye socket didn't move the blue squares. But clicking on the left eye socket (row 16, col 21) did move the blue squares.
Let me think about this more carefully. The blue squares moved horizontally when I clicked on row 16, col 21. The left blue square moved from cols 19-23 to cols 14-18 (5 columns left), and the right blue square moved from cols 39-43 to cols 44-48 (5 columns right).
So clicking on the left eye socket caused the blue squares to move apart horizontally. But clicking on the right eye socket didn't move the blue squares.
Maybe the blue squares can only move in one direction horizontally. Or maybe the blue squares are now at their maximum horizontal positions (left blue square at cols 14-18, right blue square at cols 44-48).
Let me check the black shape boundaries. The black shape is at cols 9-53. So the left blue square is at cols 14-18, which is 5 columns from the left edge of the black shape (cols 9-53). The right blue square is at cols 44-48, which is 5 columns from the right edge of the black shape (cols 9-53).
So the blue squares are near the edges of the black shape. Maybe they can't move further horizontally.
Let me try clicking on the left blue square itself to see if it moves. The left blue square is at rows 54-58, cols 14-18. Let me try clicking on row 56, col 16 (center of the left blue square).
Actually, I've been spending too many actions on exploring. Let me step back and think about the goal.
The face has two blue squares at the bottom. The blue squares can move up, down, left, and right. The goal might be to move the blue squares to specific positions to form a "face" with eyes and a mouth.
Looking at the face, the blue squares are at the bottom of the black shape. The black shape has "eye socket" regions at rows 14-18. Maybe the goal is to move the blue squares to the eye socket positions.
But the blue squares can only move up to rows 39-43 (left) and rows 44-48 (right) when they move up. They can't reach rows 14-18.
Wait, maybe I need to use MOUSE to click on the blue squares to move them to the eye socket positions. Let me try clicking on the left blue square to see if it moves to the left eye socket.
Actually, I think I need to reconsider the game mechanics. The blue squares move when I press UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, or MOUSE. The blue squares move at different speeds when I press UP or DOWN (left moves 3 rows per action, right moves 2 rows per action). The blue squares move horizontally when I press MOUSE on certain regions.
Maybe the goal is to make the blue squares align at the same row and column positions. Currently, the blue squares are at rows 54-58 (same row), but at different columns (cols 14-18 for left, cols 44-48 for right).
Let me try pressing SPACE to see if it completes the level at this position.
Actually, I've pressed SPACE multiple times, and it just counts as a meaningful action. It doesn't complete the level.
Let me think about this differently. Maybe the goal is to move the blue squares to the eye socket positions using a combination of UP/DOWN and MOUSE actions.
The eye sockets are at rows 14-18. The blue squares can only reach rows 39-43 (left) and rows 44-48 (right) when they move up. So the blue squares can't reach the eye socket positions using UP/DOWN actions.
Maybe the goal is to move the blue squares to a specific position where they form a "smile" or "frown" on the face. The blue squares are currently at rows 54-58, which is the bottom of the black shape. This is the "mouth" region of the face.
Looking at the face, the blue squares are at the bottom of the black shape, forming a "smile" or "frown". Maybe the goal is to adjust the blue squares to form a specific expression.
Let me try pressing SPACE to see if it completes the level at this position. If not, I'll try moving the blue squares to different positions.
Actually, I think I need to try a different approach. Let me look at the initial state of the game to see what the blue squares looked like at the start.
Looking at the history, the initial state had the blue squares at rows 54-58, cols 19-23 (left) and rows 54-58, cols 39-43 (right). So the blue squares were at the same row (rows 54-58), but at different columns.
After pressing UP 5 times, the blue squares moved to rows 39-43 (left) and rows 44-48 (right). After pressing DOWN 5 times, the blue squares moved back to rows 54-58 (both).
After pressing MOUSE on the left eye socket, the blue squares moved to cols 14-18 (left) and cols 44-48 (right).
So the blue squares can move up, down, and horizontally. The goal might be to move the blue squares to specific positions.
Let me try pressing SPACE to see if it completes the level at this position.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state
nodes = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
for node in nodes:
    if node['color'] == 'S':
        b = node['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"Blue square id={node['id']} bbox=({min(rows)},{min(cols)})-({max(rows)},{max(cols)})")

# Let me try pressing SPACE to see if it completes the level
action(['SPACE'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
